name: infra
description: Typed subprocess actuation: wraps the docker, git, and nix CLIs behind clean Go interfaces with structured I/O, builds immutable images from pinned Nix package sets, and provides the container-run primitive that assembles a docker run argv from binding contributions.

requirements.title: Typed subprocess interfaces
requirements.description: All external tool invocations (docker, git, nix, user-supplied resolver and data-source commands) go through typed Go interfaces backed by os/exec, using the tools' structured output modes (docker --format json, git plumbing, nix --json) — never free-text stdout scraping. Every interface accepts a context.Context and is fakeable in tests.

requirements.title: Nix image build
requirements.description: Build a template's image with Nix dockerTools.buildLayeredImage from its pinned package list; load the result into the docker daemon with a deterministic tag derived from the template name and toolset hash. The nixpkgs pin is resolved per build — the toolset's optional pin, else faber's compiled-in default — and the resolved pin folds into the toolset hash, the rendered expression, and the resolution proof (a pin-less build is byte-identical to the default). Custom binaries enter via a user-supplied overlay of derivations. No Dockerfile; no repository content in the image; packages are root-owned and immutable at runtime.

requirements.title: Package resolution proof
requirements.description: At validate time, prove every name in a template's package list resolves in the pinned nixpkgs (nix eval), without building. Unresolvable names are validate-time errors carrying the template and package name.

requirements.title: Container run primitive
requirements.description: Run one step container: assemble the docker run argv from the image, resource limits, the ordered binding contributions supplied by the security module, mounts (result dir, hook scripts, the read-only /faber/skills mount when the template declares a skills leg), and environment; when the binding set carries a file-mode secrets payload, attach the container's stdin (-i) and stream that payload in; start, stream logs, wait, and return exit code plus captured output. Supports kill on context cancellation. --rm by default; one container per step invocation, no reuse.

requirements.title: Deferred: image GC and cache eviction
requirements.description: Backlog (design edge case 11): garbage-collect Nix store paths and docker images left behind by superseded toolsets; define retention policy and a faber-owned cleanup command. First pass relies on manual docker/nix GC.

components.name: ExecAdapters
components.description: The typed CLI adapters: DockerClient, GitClient, NixClient, and CommandRunner (for opaque user commands). Each adapter maps a small set of verbs to argv, parses structured output into Go types, wraps errors with the invoked command context, and honors context deadlines.

components.name: ImageBuilder
components.description: The Nix build pipeline: renders the buildLayeredImage expression from a template's package list, overlay, and resolved nixpkgs pin (the toolset's optional per-image pin, else the compiled-in default, mapped config.PinDef -> infra.NixpkgsPin at the boundary), evaluates package resolution at validate time, builds and loads the image, and computes the deterministic tag. Owns the deferred GC/retention seam.

components.name: ContainerRunner
components.description: The single-step container lifecycle: argv assembly from image, limits, binding contributions, mounts, and env; the optional file-mode secrets payload streamed on the container's stdin; run, wait, capture, kill-on-cancel. The only place a docker run is ever constructed.

data_flows.name: Template to image to container
data_flows.description: TemplateDef packages -> nix eval proof -> buildLayeredImage -> docker load (tagged image) -> ContainerRunner argv (image + bindings + mounts) -> running step container -> exit code and captured output.
